title: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt

seo.title: Turkish Manti Recipe: Authentic Dumplings with Yogurt
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Turkish Manti, tiny flavorful dumplings served with a rich garlic yogurt sauce. This traditional Turkish Manti recipe brings a taste of Turkey to your kitchen.
longTailKeywords: best Turkish manti recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Manti Recipe with Garlic Yogurt Sauce
seo.imageAlt: Plate of authentic Turkish Manti dumplings with garlic yogurt sauce and chili oil, garnished with mint.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the taste of Turkey with these homemade Manti, delicate dumplings bathed in a creamy garlic yogurt sauce and drizzled with spiced butter.

description: Manti are delicate Turkish dumplings, hand-folded and typically filled with spiced ground meat. They are traditionally served boiled, then generously topped with a rich, garlicky yogurt sauce and a vibrant drizzle of sizzling, chili-infused butter. This iconic dish offers a delightful contrast of textures and flavors, embodying the heart of Turkish home cooking.

ingredients.notes: such as Greek yogurt or süzme yoğurt
for yogurt sauce, or to taste

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour and 1 teaspoon salt. Add the egg and gradually add water, mixing until a firm, elastic dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes until smooth. Cover with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Make the Filling
instructions.text: While the dough rests, combine ground meat, grated onion, 1 teaspoon salt, black pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes (if using) in a bowl. Mix thoroughly until well combined.

instructions.title: Roll and Cut Dough
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into 2-3 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one portion very thinly, about 1/16 inch (2mm) thick, into a large rectangle. Using a knife or pastry wheel, cut the dough into small squares, approximately 1x1 inch (2.5x2.5 cm). Repeat with remaining dough.

instructions.title: Fill and Shape Manti
instructions.text: Place a tiny amount (about 1/4 teaspoon) of the meat filling in the center of each dough square. Bring the four corners of each square together to the center and pinch them firmly to seal, forming a small pouch. Alternatively, pinch opposite corners to form a boat shape. Place shaped manti on a lightly floured tray to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Cook the Manti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully add the manti in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Cook for 8-12 minutes, or until the manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain well.

instructions.title: Prepare Sauces
instructions.text: While manti cook, whisk together yogurt, minced garlic, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a bowl for the yogurt sauce. In a small pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add 1-2 teaspoons red pepper flakes and dried mint, stirring for about 30 seconds until fragrant and the butter is sizzling. Be careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the cooked manti on serving plates. Spoon a generous amount of the garlic yogurt sauce over the manti. Drizzle the hot spiced butter over the yogurt and manti. Garnish with fresh mint or sumac if desired, and serve immediately.

tips: For truly tiny manti, which are considered more authentic, aim for dough squares no larger than 1 inch. The smaller the manti, the more skillful the cook is often perceived.
The quality of yogurt is crucial; use a thick, full-fat plain yogurt (like Greek yogurt or süzme yoğurt) for the best creamy texture and authentic taste. Avoid low-fat or sweetened varieties.
Manti are best served immediately after assembly, while they are warm and the butter sauce is still sizzling. Leftovers can be stored in the fridge, but the texture may change.
article:
Discover the culinary heart of Turkey with Manti, delicate dumplings that are much more than just pasta. Often dubbed "Turkish ravioli," Manti offers a unique textural and flavor experience, combining tiny, meat-filled dough parcels with a vibrant garlic yogurt sauce and a sizzling, aromatic butter infusion. It's a dish that embodies comfort, tradition, and the rich tapestry of Turkish gastronomy. If you're eager to try a genuine Turkish Manti Recipe, prepare for a rewarding journey into the art of making these exquisite little bites.

## What is Turkish Manti?
Turkish Manti is a beloved dish across Turkey and Central Asia, consisting of small dumplings typically filled with seasoned ground meat (usually beef or lamb). What truly sets Manti apart is its presentation: once boiled, these tender dumplings are generously topped with a cooling, garlicky yogurt sauce, followed by a spoonful of hot, melted butter infused with red pepper flakes (pul biber) and often dried mint. The contrast of hot and cold, savory and tangy, rich and fresh, creates an unforgettable symphony of flavors and textures in every bite. While regional variations exist, the most famous hails from Kayseri, where the manti are so tiny that legend says 40 of them should fit on a single spoon. This meticulous preparation is a testament to the love and care poured into this iconic Turkish dish.

## Ingredients for Turkish Manti
Creating authentic Manti requires a few key components, each playing a vital role in the dish's overall harmony.

For the Dough:
*   3 cups all-purpose flour
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/2 cup to 3/4 cup warm water (add gradually)

For the Filling:
*   1 pound ground beef or lamb (lean is preferred)
*   1 medium onion, very finely grated or minced
*   1 teaspoon salt
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes (pul biber, optional)

For the Garlic Yogurt Sauce:
*   2 cups plain yogurt (preferably full-fat, strained if possible for thickness)
*   2-3 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/2 teaspoon salt (or to taste)

For the Melted Butter Sauce:
*   4 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon red pepper flakes (pul biber)
*   1 teaspoon dried mint

For Garnish (Optional):
*   Sumac
*   Fresh mint leaves

## How to Make Turkish Manti (Step-by-Step)
Mastering the Turkish Manti Recipe involves patience and attention to detail, but the result is incredibly rewarding.

1.  **Prepare the Dough:** In a large bowl, combine flour and salt. Make a well in the center and add the egg. Gradually add warm water, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover the dough with a clean kitchen towel and let it rest for at least 30 minutes at room temperature. This resting period is crucial for a pliable dough.

2.  **Prepare the Filling:** While the dough rests, combine the ground meat, finely minced onion, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes (if using) in a bowl. Mix thoroughly with your hands until all ingredients are well incorporated. Do not overmix, but ensure the seasoning is even.

3.  **Assemble the Manti:** Divide the rested dough into 3-4 equal portions. Take one portion and roll it out on a lightly floured surface into a very thin sheet, about 1/16 to 1/8 inch thick. The thinner the dough, the more delicate your manti will be. Using a sharp knife or a pizza cutter, cut the dough into small squares, roughly 1-inch by 1-inch.

    Place a tiny amount (about 1/4 teaspoon) of the meat filling in the center of each square. Now for the signature Manti shape: bring the four corners of each square together over the filling and pinch them firmly to seal, creating a small, pyramid-like or pouch shape. Alternatively, you can fold the square in half to form a triangle, then bring the two bottom corners together and pinch them, creating a boat shape. Place the assembled manti on a lightly floured tray, ensuring they don't touch to prevent sticking. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling. This step is the most time-consuming part of the Turkish Manti Recipe but contributes to its unique charm.

4.  **Cook the Manti:** Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully add the manti in batches to avoid overcrowding the pot. Cook for 7-10 minutes, or until the manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. The exact cooking time will depend on the thickness of your dough and the size of your manti.

5.  **Prepare the Sauces:** While the manti are cooking, prepare your sauces. For the garlic yogurt, combine yogurt, minced garlic, and salt in a bowl and mix well. For the melted butter sauce, melt butter in a small pan over medium heat. Once melted and slightly fragrant, stir in the red pepper flakes and dried mint. Cook for another 30 seconds until the spices are aromatic, being careful not to burn them.

## Tips for Perfect Turkish Manti
Achieving perfectly tender and flavorful Manti requires a few insider tricks. Following this Turkish Manti Recipe carefully will yield the best results.

*   **Thin Dough is Key:** The hallmark of authentic Manti is its delicate, thin dough. Don't be afraid to roll it out as thinly as possible without tearing. This ensures the dumplings cook quickly and have a melt-in-your-mouth texture.
*   **Small is Traditional:** While time-consuming, making the manti tiny (especially for Kayseri style) results in a more elegant dish where the filling and sauce ratio is perfect in every bite.
*   **Don't Overfill:** A common mistake is adding too much filling. A small amount is sufficient; otherwise, the manti will be hard to seal and may burst during cooking.
*   **Rest the Dough:** The resting period for the dough is non-negotiable. It allows the gluten to relax, making the dough easier to roll out thinly without snapping back.
*   **Fresh Ingredients:** Use fresh, high-quality ground meat and full-fat plain yogurt for the best flavor and texture. Freshly minced garlic is also crucial for the sauce.
*   **Prevent Sticking:** Ensure your work surface and trays are well-floured when handling the dough and placing the assembled manti.

## Serving Suggestions for Manti
Manti is traditionally served hot, immediately after cooking, to ensure the dumplings are tender and the sauces vibrant. Ladle the cooked manti into individual serving bowls. Spoon a generous amount of the garlic yogurt sauce over the top, allowing it to coat the dumplings. Finally, drizzle the hot, sizzling red pepper and mint infused butter over the yogurt. The contrast of the cool yogurt and the hot, spicy butter is what makes Manti truly special. For an extra touch of flavor and color, sprinkle with sumac and a few fresh mint leaves. Manti is hearty enough to be a main course on its own. A glass of chilled Ayran (a traditional savory yogurt drink) makes an excellent accompaniment, perfectly complementing the rich flavors of the dish.

## Variations and Additions
While the classic Turkish Manti Recipe is beloved, there are several regional variations and creative additions you can explore.

*   **Vegetarian Manti:** For a meat-free alternative, the filling can be made with finely diced potatoes, lentils, spinach, or even mushrooms, seasoned with similar spices.
*   **Tomato Paste in Butter Sauce:** Some recipes add a teaspoon of tomato paste to the melted butter sauce, simmering it for a minute before adding the red pepper flakes and mint. This adds a subtle tang and deeper color.
*   **Baked Manti (Sini Manti):** In some regions, especially around Gaziantep, manti are sometimes baked in a large pan (sini) until golden brown before having broth and yogurt sauce added. This gives the dumplings a slightly crispier texture.
*   **Different Spices:** Experiment with adding a pinch of cumin, allspice, or dried basil to the meat filling for a different aromatic profile.
*   **Broth:** Occasionally, a little of the manti cooking water or chicken broth is added to the serving bowl before the sauces, creating a slightly soupy consistency.

Manti is more than just a dish; it's a culinary experience deeply woven into the fabric of Turkish culture. The effort involved in preparing this Turkish Manti Recipe is a labor of love that is richly rewarded with every flavorful bite. So, why not embark on making this delightful Turkish Manti Recipe today and bring a taste of Turkey into your home?
